inflation.label: oil + tariffs
inflation.note: Pre-conflict he saw tariff goods inflation and housing fading toward 2 percent; now the Iran oil shock dominates the risk, threatening to keep core near 3 percent and unanchor expectations.
inflation.quote: Tariffs had clearly pushed up goods inflation, but that increase likely would have waned during 2026 as prices fully adjusted to the new tariff regime.

labor.label: stable / balanced
labor.note: Describes the labor market as lukewarm but largely stable, with unemployment near 4.3 percent, somewhat above his estimate of maximum employment.
labor.quote: the labor market appeared lukewarm but largely stable with the unemployment rate having hovered around 4.3 percent since May 2025

stance.label: somewhat/modestly restrictive
stance.note: Views policy as mildly restrictive, which pre-conflict justified further gradual cuts, but the oil shock now argues for signaling the next move could be a cut or a hike.
stance.quote: Given that I viewed policy as mildly restrictive, some further cuts to the federal funds rate would likely be appropriate over time.
